content_of_post_to_answer: Is modern life actually more stressful than life 30 years ago?
answer: The comparison is tricky because stress has changed shape more than it has simply increased. Thirty years ago, many people faced heavier physical burdens and fewer conveniences, but modern life adds a different kind of pressure: constant comparison, endless information, and the feeling that we should always be available. The hidden cost of progress is that problems no longer leave us alone; they follow us through our devices. Modern stress is often less about survival and more about never mentally switching off.
